reviews.review_msg: Loved the content for the course, as I did HIST 115 last term, but the grading in this class was horrific. It took forever to be graded, and the marks I got were never followed up with ANY feedback, and when I reached out to my TA there was zero elaboration. Fung seemed nice, but getting a TA in their courses is like playing russian roulette.

Marking is extremely subjective. Feedback is also very vague so you're unsure why you lost marks. I might've just had a really bad TA this semester, because the prof seems nice and the content is actually interesting. Would not take this class with this prof unfortunately

The grading is insanely subjective and provides zero feedback. It takes weeks for anything to be marked. We got our project marked that we started in the middle of the term AFTER THE FINAL WAS DONE. There is no way to get help for your work because everything will be marked so late so you have no way of knowing how you're doing in the term. avoid!!

Not a good course I'm not even sure how this is a first year level course. Grades extremely harshly without giving any outline. Weekly activity and quiz were easy but only count for a small % of your mark. Do not take this course if you're going to use it as a GPA booster, it tanked my GPA.
Easy to follow lectures and very engaging, just an incredibly hard marker! Rarely gives out 90%+, but feedback is consistently insightful.

Super easy, instructions were clear. I never watched any of the lectures, the lecture notes that are provided. Quizzes are easy and straightforward/ GPA booster. But be specific when selecting your topic. I spent maybe a hour a week on the course. Exam was simple and easy and were able to do online with plenty of time.
genuinely how is this class real. I have no words just don't even consider taking this I wish I saved myself a semester from hell

Had an assignment that required a "unique" topic, chose an unique topic, got marks deducted due to this topic being already discussed sometime in the past. Start researching for this topic, can't find a single article related to my topic. Found 2 tangentially related articles in German, got further marks deducted for not using those articles.

Lectures were recorded by someone else, didn't have a problem with that, it was fine. TA's mark learning activities, quizzes not hard if you do the lecture. Midterm was hard (2 hours to complete time limit) and marked roughly. Had a few problems submitting and Dr. Fung helped me out and was accepting a late submission upon proof of date editing.

As others have stated, the grading in this class is outrageous. I took this as an elective thinking it would be easy IT IS NOT! Worst grades I've ever gotten and it's a 100 level course? TA's mark everything and lectures are recorded by someone else so this is more of a review of the class rather than the prof. GPA destroyer for sure

Probably the worst class I have ever taken at SFU. I'm a fourth-year student who is getting marked harshly for not writing more when I exactly wrote the same amount and structure as the example that is IN THE CANVAS. It seems like TAs have their own grading subjectivity, as they give random feedback that doesn't align with the work.

condescending when asked where points were lost on assignment/midterm. Unclear rubrics, even if you follow them you are likely to get a bad grade. Hard and subjective marking, I wish I could have dropped this class before it lowered my GPA
don't take this class. Grading so unfair and so unclear even if you meet all criteria. Don't even think about taking this class. Literally a 100 level class and graded like it was 400.

The marking is extremely subjective as other students have stated. The rubrics are very unclear. The TA's grade everything. The replies I received when asking for explanations were condescending and  unhelpful. If you are uncomfortable interacting with AI stay away from this class. Terrible experience.
Just avoid. Seriously don't even consider this class. Midterm was 5 questions that were marked so unfairly. Weekly readings that made no sense. Worst prof. Just avoid at all costs honestly.
The marking in this course is extremely subjective, and the rubric does not reflect the assignment criteria.
Stay away as soon as you see her name for the sake of your own good. Horrible course.

JUST AVOID AVOID. No clear grading criteria. You are definitely going to lose marks even if you write the best answer. Ridiculously hard for a 100 level course. Simply stay away as soon as you see this prof.

Just avoid this course with this prof. Horrible experience. Prof doesn't teach anything and full course is basically copied from other instructors. Exams are literally a joke, having 5 questions worth 20 marks in midterm and despite you write the best answer, you are surely going to lose points for not writing the way they want. Simply avoid.
This class was boring and had a lot of work.

This is the worst online class ever taken. 5 short written questions on exam worth 20 points each, no explanation of how they want it / where the 20 points are coming from. horrible average for midterm. DO not take this class. extremely short lecture videos/ small amount of weekly work but exams/assignments graded impossibly.
Course is organized very poorly, grading is extremely subjective. Very bad experience in this course and just wanted to learn about the topic but I regret this now. do not take.
Dismissive and unhelpful

This class was like academic-citation boot camp. It's a lot of work and the grading is tough and technical + lack of contact with the professor (online class). I felt I learned more about MLA 9th than I did gender issues. I took the class out of casual interest and was punished for it. If it's not required for your degree, I wouldn't recommend.

Great material, but subjective marking. Two-part exams: first part - two terms you have to define. No rubric; you just have to pray that you guess what the professor wanted. Second part - essay. No feedback after grading, just an arbitrary rubric that, AGAIN, is released after the exam. If you're consodering to take it as an elective, please don't

As others have mentioned, her TAs are quite difficult graders for a first year course. I was taking third year sociology courses at the same time and receiving much better marks. It was a bit of a deterrent for me for engaging with more GSWS courses, as what is expected overall is just a bit unclear.

Carmen is a great prof. However, the issues lay within the TA's. My TA marked very subjectively, and would deduct marks for things not asked for in the questions. If your work didn't align with their opinions, marks were deducted as well it seems. Rubric was released after assignments were handed back, which made it hard to know what was wanted.

I did 100 with them, criteria was clear, assignments were fair, exams were fair. Now in 101, rubric is incredibly subjective, only revealed rubric after the exam had been graded. Exam material focuses on one specific module. Strict criteria for one 100 word question, yet only revealed this after the fact. Prof is passionate, do 100 as a gpa booster

Very passionate and knowledgeable about the topic and engages in amazing discussions.I respect her a lot. Got a better mark on the final written assignment as I tried to use some of her optional readings in my writing fyi. Attendance and participation matters but she will never make you speak if you don't want to. Understanding and deeply caring.

Dr. Fung is amazing! Also had a first year course with her, mainly marked hard by TAs and why she has lower ratings. Extremely knowledgable on the topic and her lectures are engaging. She does not believe in exams and has lots of readings, essays and attendance marks, does not make you talk if you don't want to. Very approachable and understanding.
